entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into a form usable by plants.

entities.name: ORGANIC MANURES
entities.description: Organic materials used as fertilizer to improve soil fertility and productivity.

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of maintaining or improving the nutrient status and health of soil.

entities.name: LEGUMINOUS CROPS
entities.description: Crops from the legume family that can fix nitrogen in the soil.

entities.name: SOIL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of the soil to support crop growth and yield.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A critical nutrient transferred from legume crops to associated and succeeding crops.

entities.name: FARMYARD MANURE
entities.description: A specific type of organic manure derived from livestock waste.

entities.name: UNDECOMPOSED CROP RESIDUE
entities.description: A specific type of organic manure consisting of plant materials left after harvest.

entities.name: GREEN MANURE
entities.description: A specific type of organic manure consisting of green plant material plowed into the soil.

entities.name: LOCATION
entities.description: The geographic area affecting the response of crops to organic manure.

entities.name: TIME
entities.description: The temporal factor affecting the response of crops to organic manure.

entities.name: METHOD OF APPLICATION
entities.description: The technique used to apply organic manure to the soil.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS
entities.description: External factors that influence the effectiveness of organic manure.

entities.name: SOIL PROPERTIES
entities.description: The physical, chemical, and biological characteristics of the soil.
